---
title: "Berant on Complex Question Answering @ NAACL 2022"
description: "Jonathan Berant at SUKI: of decomposition / retrieval / reasoning, retrieval is the hardest step — surprising more than one of us."
author: "synesis"
date: "2022-07-14"
categories: [NLP, knowledge graphs, conference, NAACL]
image: cover.jpg
---

::: {layout-ncol=2}
![SUKI Workshop @ NAACL 2022. Image from [SUKI workshop](https://suki-workshop.github.io/).](cover.jpg)

![Slide from Berant's talk at the [SUKI workshop](https://suki-workshop.github.io/).](02.jpg)

![Slide from Berant's talk at the [SUKI workshop](https://suki-workshop.github.io/).](03.jpg)

![Slide from Berant's talk at the [SUKI workshop](https://suki-workshop.github.io/).](04.jpg)
:::
Professor Jonathan Berant (Tel Aviv University) gave an invited talk at SUKI Workshop @ NAACL2022 on complex question answering. He described among the three steps — question decomposition, retrieval and reasoning — the second step is particularly important and hard.

More than one of us was surprised that the step of question decomposition/implicit relation detection is not the most difficult one.

SUKI Workshop @ NAACL2022: <https://suki-workshop.github.io/>


*Originally posted on [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/benjaminhan_naacl2022-naacl2022-knowledgegraph-activity-6953587770636271616-IrQX).*
